claim: 10-20% of people with severe mental disorder receive no treatment in low and middle income countries.

result.claim: 10-20% of people with severe mental disorder receive no treatment in low and middle income countries.
result.reasoning: The claim states that 10-20% of people with severe mental disorder receive no treatment in low and middle-income countries. However, evidence from the World Health Organization World Mental Health Surveys (Paper ID: 6490571) directly refutes this. Proposition ID: prop_1789 and the abstract of paper 6490571 clearly state that "Between 76.3% and 85.4% of serious cases in less-developed countries received no treatment in the 12 months before the interview." This indicates a significantly higher percentage of untreated individuals than the claim suggests. The study was a face-to-face household survey across 14 countries (6 less developed, 8 developed), providing a robust basis for these findings.
